terms.management_clue: On chromosome 20q13.32 encodes the alpha subunit of the stimulatory G protein

terms.etiology_clue: Accumulation of fluid between the reduced enamel epithelium and crown of an unerupted tooth; most common developmental odontogenic cyst
terms.management_clue: Enucleation with extraction of associated impacted tooth; marsupialisation followed by enucleation for large cysts in growing patients
Encodes a receptor tyrosine kinase (170 kDa) critical for cell signaling in neural crest development, cell differentiation, and growth.
A critical tumor suppressor gene located on chromosome 9, encoding a receptor that regulates cell growth by inhibiting the Sonic Hedgehog (SHH) signaling pathway.

terms.etiology_clue: Hamartomatous odontogenic malformation; compound type resembles rudimentary teeth, complex type forms disorganised calcified mass; most common odontogenic tumour
terms.management_clue: Conservative surgical excision; extraction of associated impacted tooth if present; excellent prognosis with negligible recurrence risk

Somatic gene alteration amino acid valine replaced by glutamic acid at position 600 in the BRAF protein. This change causes activation of the MAPK/ERK pathway, driving tumor growth, cell proliferation, and cancer survival.
Conservative surgical excision; extraction of associated impacted tooth if present; excellent prognosis with negligible recurrence risk
